name: From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular
name_original: De Encinas Reales al Río Anzur — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Encinas Reales offers diverse terrain for runners. The region features a landscape characterized by olive groves, river valleys, and rolling hills, providing varied elevation changes. These routes often include paths through agricultural areas and along natural waterways, suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Encinas Reales The most popular jogging route is Cerrillo de la Buitrera Loop from Rute, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 56 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain, making it a challenging option for experienced runners. Another top favourite among local runners is From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This route offers a scenic run along the Río Anzur, with gentle inclines and varied natural surroundings. Local runners also love the Castillo de Gómez Arias — circular desde Benamejí, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through historical areas and local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Encinas Reales is defined by paths through olive groves, riverbanks, and undulating hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging running routes. The routes in Encinas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Encinas Reales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Encinas Reales?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Encinas Reales, offering diverse terrain for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Encinas Reales?
A: Yes, while many routes feature rolling hills, there is at least one easy route available. For a moderate option that's still accessible, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rute – Rute loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 9.5 km with gentle elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Encinas Reales?
A: Jogging routes in Encinas Reales typically feature paths through olive groves, along riverbanks, and over undulating hills. You'll encounter varied elevation changes, from gentle inclines to more challenging climbs, often passing through agricultural areas and natural waterways.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Encinas Reales area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Encinas Reales are circular. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287172524/from-encinas-reales-to-rio-anzur-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering a scenic 7.7 km run along the river.

Q: What are some points of interest or landmarks I might see while running in Encinas Reales?
A: The region offers several interesting sights. You might encounter historical areas like those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287208757/castillo-de-gomez-arias-circular-desde-benameji" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Gómez Arias&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Araceli&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1853196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cedrón Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or offer views?
A: Many routes provide scenic views, especially those traversing the rolling hills and river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287172524/from-encinas-reales-to-rio-anzur-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its beautiful riverside scenery and varied natural surroundings.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Encinas Reales?
A: The running routes in Encinas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the olive groves, and the peaceful atmosphere of the river paths.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1288090761/cerrillo-de-la-buitrera-loop-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrillo de la Buitrera Loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 6.5 km. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287223600/from-encinas-reales-to-benameji-circular-by-the-camino-de-los-olivares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Benamejí — circular by the Camino de los Olivares&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15.7 km trail.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by water features or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by natural waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287172524/from-encinas-reales-to-rio-anzur-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers a beautiful run along the Río Anzur.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Encinas Reales?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate of the region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are advisable if running during this season. Winters are mild, making them suitable for running as well.

Q: Are there any running trails that are particularly good for wildlife spotting?
A: Routes that follow riverbanks or pass through less developed agricultural areas, such as those along the Río Anzur, often provide opportunities for wildlife spotting. Keep an eye out for local birdlife and other small animals native to the Andalusian countryside.
tips: A beautiful town that is the beginning of the 9. stage of the Camino de Santiago - Camino Mozárabe de Málaga. Near the Ermita del Calvario there is a free hostel for pilgrims. The keys are found at the local police office.
